mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in supply capacity that reduces production costs and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of supply-side shocks on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals an improvement in labor market conditions that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in recovery periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of labor market conditions on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in demand for residential construction that increases economic activity and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of demand-side shocks on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer spending that increases economic activity and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of demand-side shocks on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a contraction in supply capacity that increases production costs and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of supply-side shocks on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of labor market conditions on equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the impact of monetary policy on equity returns.
